names.japanese_name: マイクロクリスタリンワックス
names.japanese_label_name: マイクロクリスタリンワックス

natural_occurrence.description: A refined petroleum-based wax with smaller, more irregular crystal structure than paraffin wax. More flexible and has better adhesive properties than paraffin. Derived from crude oil refining.

manufacturing.description: Extracted during the petroleum refining process from heavier oil fractions. The crude wax is de-oiled, filtered, and purified through solvent extraction and hydrotreating to produce food-grade microcrystalline wax.

other_uses.household: Modeling clay, ski wax, surfboard wax

eu.conditions: Only highly refined food-grade wax authorized for surface treatment

usa.notes: Approved as masticatory substance in chewing gum base and protective coating

japan.japanese_regulation_name: マイクロクリスタリンワックス

australia_nz.notes: Permitted glazing agent (subdivided into E905a, E905b, E905c variants)

canada.reference: Lists of Permitted Glazing and Polishing Agents

safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: Not specified (no safety concern at current use levels)

regulatory_history.event: JECFA evaluates petroleum waxes for food use
EFSA re-evaluates food-grade petroleum waxes and confirms safety

adi_context.source_note: No ADI established - microcrystalline wax is not absorbed by the digestive system and passes through unchanged.

fun_facts: Made from petroleum - the same source as gasoline and motor oil
More flexible than regular paraffin wax due to smaller crystal structure
Used in both your chewing gum and your cheese coating
Also found in crayons, lipstick, and ski wax
Passes through your digestive system completely unchanged
Must be highly refined to food-grade quality (removes carcinogens)
